summary: Normalizes a POSIX-style filesystem string while preserving whether it is absolute and whether it ends with a separator.
signature_intent: Accepts one filesystem-style string and returns its normalized POSIX-style form.
behavior: Validates that the input is an acceptable filesystem string.
Returns the current-directory marker for an empty relative input.
Preserves a leading root separator for absolute inputs.
Simplifies redundant separators and dot segments according to POSIX normalization rules.
Resolves parent-directory segments where normalization permits it.
Preserves a trailing separator when the original non-empty input ended with one.
Returns a relative normalized result for relative inputs and an absolute normalized result for absolute inputs.
edge_cases: Empty input returns the current-directory marker.
A relative input that normalizes to empty returns the current-directory marker.
An absolute input that normalizes to empty returns the root separator.
A non-empty input ending in a separator returns a normalized result that also ends in a separator.
forbidden_details: Internal helper names, local identifiers, source locations, and character-code implementation details are intentionally omitted.
